az-500.title: AZ-500 Microsoft Azure Security Technologies Practice Exam
az-500.description: Microsoft Certified: Azure Security Engineer Associate

topics.name: Manage identity and access
modules: Secure Azure solutions with Azure Active Directory
Configure Azure AD privileged identity management

topics.name: Manage security operations
modules: Configure and manage security monitoring and automation

questions.question: Which control enforces multi-factor authentication only when a sign-in risk is high?
options: Conditional Access with sign-in risk policy
questions.explanation: Conditional Access evaluates risk and enforces MFA dynamically.

reasoning.correct: Risk-based CA policies provide adaptive access control.

why_others_wrong.1: Relates to PIM, not sign-in risk.

questions.question: You need outbound FQDN filtering and DNAT/SNAT for workloads in rg-hr-test-101. Which should you deploy?
questions.explanation: Azure Firewall provides L3–L7 filtering, FQDN rules, and NAT.

reasoning.correct: Centralized network security with full features for egress/ingress control.

why_others_wrong.1: Stateful 5-tuple filtering; no FQDN or NAT.
why_others_wrong.2: Global entry; not a VNet firewall.

questions.question: To restrict Key Vault access to private network only, which feature should you use?
options: Public endpoint with IP ACL only
questions.explanation: Private endpoints route traffic through a private IP in your VNet.

reasoning.correct: Eliminates exposure to public internet while enabling secure access.

why_others_wrong.1: Help with NSG rules but do not privatize the service.

questions.question: Which Defender for Cloud capabilities help reduce attack surface? (Select two)
questions.explanation: Posture recommendations and JIT VM access reduce exposure and harden workloads.

reasoning.correct: They are proactive controls and protections for resources.

questions.question: Which plan adds adaptive real-time DDoS mitigation for VNet resources?
questions.explanation: DDoS Protection Standard provides adaptive tuning and mitigation.

reasoning.correct: Protects public endpoints in a VNet at network edge.

why_others_wrong.0: L7 HTTP(S) protection; not volumetric network DDoS.
why_others_wrong.1: Firewall does not provide DDoS network scrubbing.

questions.explanation: Private endpoints route traffic through a private IP in your VNet.

reasoning.correct: Eliminates exposure to public internet while enabling secure access.

why_others_wrong.3: Help with NSG rules but do not privatize the service.

questions.question: Which Defender for Cloud capabilities help reduce attack surface? (Select two)
questions.explanation: Posture recommendations and JIT VM access reduce exposure and harden workloads.

reasoning.correct: They are proactive controls and protections for resources.

questions.question: Which plan adds adaptive real-time DDoS mitigation for VNet resources?
questions.explanation: DDoS Protection Standard provides adaptive tuning and mitigation.

reasoning.correct: Protects public endpoints in a VNet at network edge.

why_others_wrong.1: L7 HTTP(S) protection; not volumetric network DDoS.
why_others_wrong.3: Firewall does not provide DDoS network scrubbing.

questions.question: Which control enforces multi-factor authentication only when a sign-in risk is high?
options: Conditional Access with sign-in risk policy
questions.explanation: Conditional Access evaluates risk and enforces MFA dynamically.

reasoning.correct: Risk-based CA policies provide adaptive access control.

why_others_wrong.1: Relates to PIM, not sign-in risk.

questions.question: You need outbound FQDN filtering and DNAT/SNAT for workloads in rg-hr-dev-271. Which should you deploy?
questions.explanation: Azure Firewall provides L3–L7 filtering, FQDN rules, and NAT.

reasoning.correct: Centralized network security with full features for egress/ingress control.

why_others_wrong.1: Stateful 5-tuple filtering; no FQDN or NAT.
why_others_wrong.3: Global entry; not a VNet firewall.

reasoning.correct: They are proactive controls and protections for resources.

questions.question: Which plan adds adaptive real-time DDoS mitigation for VNet resources?
questions.explanation: DDoS Protection Standard provides adaptive tuning and mitigation.

reasoning.correct: Protects public endpoints in a VNet at network edge.

why_others_wrong.1: L7 HTTP(S) protection; not volumetric network DDoS.
why_others_wrong.3: Firewall does not provide DDoS network scrubbing.

questions.question: Which control enforces multi-factor authentication only when a sign-in risk is high?
options: Conditional Access with sign-in risk policy
questions.explanation: Conditional Access evaluates risk and enforces MFA dynamically.

reasoning.correct: Risk-based CA policies provide adaptive access control.

why_others_wrong.0: Relates to PIM, not sign-in risk.

questions.question: You need outbound FQDN filtering and DNAT/SNAT for workloads in rg-hr-prod-606. Which should you deploy?
questions.explanation: Azure Firewall provides L3–L7 filtering, FQDN rules, and NAT.

reasoning.correct: Centralized network security with full features for egress/ingress control.

why_others_wrong.0: Stateful 5-tuple filtering; no FQDN or NAT.
why_others_wrong.2: Global entry; not a VNet firewall.

questions.question: To restrict Key Vault access to private network only, which feature should you use?
options: Public endpoint with IP ACL only
questions.explanation: Private endpoints route traffic through a private IP in your VNet.

reasoning.correct: Eliminates exposure to public internet while enabling secure access.

why_others_wrong.0: Help with NSG rules but do not privatize the service.

reasoning.correct: They are proactive controls and protections for resources.

questions.question: Which plan adds adaptive real-time DDoS mitigation for VNet resources?
questions.explanation: DDoS Protection Standard provides adaptive tuning and mitigation.

reasoning.correct: Protects public endpoints in a VNet at network edge.

why_others_wrong.0: Firewall does not provide DDoS network scrubbing.
why_others_wrong.3: L7 HTTP(S) protection; not volumetric network DDoS.

questions.question: Which control enforces multi-factor authentication only when a sign-in risk is high?
options: Conditional Access with sign-in risk policy
questions.explanation: Conditional Access evaluates risk and enforces MFA dynamically.

reasoning.correct: Risk-based CA policies provide adaptive access control.

why_others_wrong.0: Relates to PIM, not sign-in risk.

questions.question: You need outbound FQDN filtering and DNAT/SNAT for workloads in rg-hr-prod-126. Which should you deploy?
questions.explanation: Azure Firewall provides L3–L7 filtering, FQDN rules, and NAT.

reasoning.correct: Centralized network security with full features for egress/ingress control.

why_others_wrong.0: Stateful 5-tuple filtering; no FQDN or NAT.
why_others_wrong.3: Global entry; not a VNet firewall.

questions.question: To restrict Key Vault access to private network only, which feature should you use?
options: Public endpoint with IP ACL only
questions.explanation: Private endpoints route traffic through a private IP in your VNet.

reasoning.correct: Eliminates exposure to public internet while enabling secure access.

why_others_wrong.3: Help with NSG rules but do not privatize the service.

certificationInfo.title: Microsoft Certified: Azure Security Engineer Associate
certificationInfo.description: This certification validates your expertise in implementing security controls and threat protection, managing identity and access, and protecting data, applications, and networks in cloud and hybrid environments.
prerequisites: Experience with Azure workloads and security controls
Knowledge of security concepts and methodologies
Experience with identity and access management
Understanding of network security and cloud security

skills: Configure Azure Active Directory for workloads
Configure Azure AD Privileged Identity Management
Configure and manage security monitoring solutions
Configure security policies to manage data
Configure encryption for data at rest
Configure encryption for data in transit
